Examination.ID: Unique identifier for each examination record, ensuring data integrity.
Examination.Examination Date: Date when the examination was conducted, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.
Examination.aCL IgG: Measurement of aCL IgG antibodies, indicating potential autoimmune activity.
Examination.aCL IgM: Measurement of aCL IgM antibodies, relevant for diagnosing certain autoimmune conditions.
Examination.ANA: Numeric score representing antinuclear antibody test results, indicating autoimmune disease presence.
Examination.ANA Pattern: Descriptive pattern of ANA test results, aiding in specific autoimmune diagnosis.
Examination.aCL IgA: Measurement of aCL IgA antibodies, contributing to autoimmune disease assessment.
Examination.Diagnosis: Clinical diagnosis associated with the examination, providing insight into patient condition.
Examination.KCT: Result of the KCT test, indicating potential clotting abnormalities.
Examination.RVVT: Result of the RVVT test, assessing the presence of clotting factor deficiencies.
Examination.LAC: Indicates lactate levels, reflecting metabolic state and potential tissue hypoxia.
Examination.Symptoms: Lists patient symptoms, aiding in diagnosis and treatment planning.
Examination.Thrombosis: Represents severity of thrombosis, guiding clinical decisions and interventions.

Patient.ID: Unique numeric identifier for each patient, ensuring distinct records.
Patient.SEX: Gender of the patient, represented as 'F' for female or 'M' for male.
Patient.Birthday: Patient's birth date, crucial for age calculation and demographic analysis.
Patient.Description: Date associated with a specific event or record related to the patient.
Patient.First Date: Initial date of interaction or treatment for the patient, important for tracking.
Patient.Admission: Status of patient admission, indicating whether they are admitted or not.
Patient.Diagnosis: Medical condition or disease identified for the patient, essential for treatment planning.

Laboratory.ID: Unique identifier for each laboratory record, ensuring data integrity.
Laboratory.Date: Date of the laboratory test, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.
Laboratory.GOT: Glutamate oxaloacetate transaminase level, indicating liver function.
Laboratory.GPT: Glutamate pyruvate transaminase level, a marker for liver health.
Laboratory.LDH: Lactate dehydrogenase level, used to assess tissue damage.
Laboratory.ALP: Alkaline phosphatase level, important for liver and bone health.
Laboratory.TP: Total protein level in blood, reflecting nutritional status.
Laboratory.ALB: Albumin level, indicating liver function and nutritional state.
Laboratory.UA: Uric acid level, relevant for gout and kidney function.
Laboratory.UN: Urea nitrogen level, used to evaluate kidney function.
Laboratory.CRE: Measures creatinine levels, indicating kidney function; numeric values range from 0.1 to 17.1.
Laboratory.T-BIL: Indicates total bilirubin levels, reflecting liver function; values range from 0.1 to 7.9.
Laboratory.T-CHO: Represents total cholesterol levels, important for cardiovascular health; values range from 37 to 568.
Laboratory.TG: Measures triglyceride levels, assessing fat metabolism; values range from 1 to 867.
Laboratory.CPK: Indicates creatine phosphokinase levels, related to muscle damage; values range from 0 to 10835.
Laboratory.GLU: Measures glucose levels, crucial for diabetes management; values range from 62 to 499.
Laboratory.WBC: Indicates white blood cell count, reflecting immune system health; values range from 0.9 to 35.2.
Laboratory.RBC: Measures red blood cell count, important for oxygen transport; values range from 0.4 to 6.6.
Laboratory.HGB: Indicates hemoglobin levels, essential for oxygen delivery; values range from 1.3 to 18.9.
Laboratory.HCT: Measures hematocrit levels, indicating blood volume percentage; values range from 3.0 to 56.0.
Laboratory.PLT: Platelet count indicating blood clotting ability, with a wide numeric range.
Laboratory.PT: Prothrombin time measurement reflecting blood coagulation efficiency, formatted as a real number.
Laboratory.APTT: Activated partial thromboplastin time indicating blood clotting function, primarily numeric values.
Laboratory.FG: Fibrinogen level measurement essential for assessing blood clotting, represented as a real number.
Laboratory.PIC: Patient identification code for laboratory results, formatted as a three-digit numeric identifier.
Laboratory.TAT: Turnaround time for laboratory tests, indicating efficiency in processing results, numeric values.
Laboratory.TAT2: Secondary turnaround time measurement for tests, providing additional efficiency insights, numeric format.
Laboratory.U-PRO: Urine protein level indicator with various distinct values, reflecting kidney function.
Laboratory.IGG: Immunoglobulin G level measurement, crucial for immune response assessment, with a broad numeric range.
Laboratory.IGA: Immunoglobulin A level measurement, important for mucosal immunity, formatted as a three-digit code.
Laboratory.IGM: Indicates immunoglobulin M levels, reflecting immune response strength.
Laboratory.CRP: C-reactive protein levels, indicating inflammation or infection severity.
Laboratory.RA: Rheumatoid arthritis test results, assessing autoimmune disease presence.
Laboratory.RF: Rheumatoid factor levels, aiding in diagnosing rheumatoid arthritis.
Laboratory.C3: Complement component 3 levels, important for immune system function.
Laboratory.C4: Complement component 4 levels, related to immune response and inflammation.
Laboratory.RNP: Ribonucleoprotein antibodies, associated with autoimmune disorders.
Laboratory.SM: Smith antibodies, indicating potential systemic lupus erythematosus.
Laboratory.SC170: Specific antibodies related to autoimmune conditions, indicating disease presence.
Laboratory.SSA: Anti-Sjögren's syndrome-related antigen A antibodies, linked to autoimmune diseases.
Laboratory.SSB: Indicates sample status with limited distinct values, mostly unrecorded.
Laboratory.CENTROMEA: Represents centromere measurement, predominantly missing data with few outcomes.
Laboratory.DNA: Quantifies DNA concentration with a wide range of numeric values.
Laboratory.DNA-II: Currently holds no data, indicating no recorded measurements.
